chunks.text: ORDER SHEET THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANO Cr. Misc. Appln. No. S-66 of 2026 Date Orders with signature of Judge 1. For orders on M.A No. 1004/2026. (Urgency Application) 2. For order on office objection. 3. For orders on M.A No. 1005/2026. (Exemption Application) 4. For hearing of main case. 5. For orders on M.A No. 1006/2026. 02-03-2026 M/s Idrees Ahmed Mangi and Abdul Jabbar Shaikh, advocates for the applicants..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. 1. Urgency application is disposed of. 2. One week's time is granted to comply with office objection. 3. Exemption is granted subject to all just legal exceptions. 4 & 5. Learned counsel for the applicants submits that civil disputes are pending between the parties. He contends that prior to the present proceedings, the mother of respondent No.4 had filed Criminal Case No. 206/2026 in respect of the same incident alleged to have taken place on the same date and time, wherein it was alleged that the present applicants, along with 15 unidentified persons armed with weapons, trespassed into her house situated at Darri Muhalla, committed theft of thirty lac rupees and attempted to unlawfully occupy the property. The said application for registration of F.I.R. was dismissed vide order dated 23.02.2026 and protection was granted to the mother of respondent No.4. Learned counsel further submits that another application bearing No. 207/2026, filed by respondent No.4, was also dismissed vide order dated 23.02.2026. It is argued that the sequence of events reflects existence of a civil dispute between the parties and that the impugned order dated 28.02.2026 has been passed without properly appreciating these material aspects.

Learned counsel further submits that another application bearing No. 207/2026, filed by respondent No.4, was also dismissed vide order dated 23.02.2026. It is argued that the sequence of events reflects existence of a civil dispute between the parties and that the impugned order dated 28.02.2026 has been passed without properly appreciating these material aspects. He therefore prays for suspension of the impugned order. Heard. On tentative assessment of the submissions made at the bar and the material placed before this Court, it appears that the controversy arises out of a dispute which prima facie involves civil elements, and the sequence of prior proceedings requires examination. The matter thus warrants consideration. Accordingly, issue notice to the respondents as well as to the learned Additional Prosecutor General. Meanwhile, to preserve the subject matter and to avoid multiplicity of proceedings, the impugned order dated 28.02.2026 shall remain suspended till the next date of hearing. To come up on 09.03.2026. Both applicants shall remain present on the next date. Judge Abdul Salam/P.A ****
